meaning: n.计量杯,小玩意,[机]盘车,小帆船
examples: Jigger machine is used extensively in the field of printing and dyeing.
The paper expounded that cotton fabric was dyed with direct and reactive dyes in VGJ jumbo jigger imported from Italy.
A two-masted fore - and-aft-rigged sailing vessel similar to the ketch but having a smaller jigger mast stepped abaft the rudder.
The idea is to re-jigger the numbers to get that level of troops up to about 160, 000.
Some naive homebuyers paid too much and now we have to re-jigger things.
